name: Hoz del Conde and Villaconejos de Tr
name_original: Villaconejos de Trabaque y Albalate de las Noqueras por la Hoz del Conde — circular desde Cañamares
description: This difficult 23.9-mile touring cycling route through the Serranía de Cuenca explores the Hoz del Conde, featuring limestone gorges and for
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cañamares offers routes through the natural landscapes of the Serranía de Cuenca, characterized by the Escabas River. The region features impressive gorges, rocky outcrops, and extensive forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often follow river valleys, passing through areas of significant ecological value. This area provides a mix of elevations, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging climbs within the mountainous terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Cañamares The most popular touring cycling route is Priego to Cañamares via Estrecho de Priego Trail, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Escabas River through a notable gorge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hoz del Conde and Villaconejos de Tr, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and scenic views through the Hoz del Conde gorge. Local touring cyclists also love the Old Fuertescusa Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through the Fuertescusa area, often completed in about 46 minutes. This easy route offers a less strenuous option for exploring the local landscape. Touring cycling around Cañamares is defined by river valleys, gorges, and forested areas within the Serranía de Cuenca. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Cañamares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cañamares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cañamares?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Cañamares, offering a variety of experiences through the natural landscapes of the Serranía de Cuenca. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy paths to more challenging rides with significant climbs.

Q: What are some easier touring cycling routes for beginners in Cañamares?
A: For those seeking a less strenuous ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093963639/old-fuertescusa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Fuertescusa Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This easy 7.6-mile (12.3 km) route leads through the Fuertescusa area, offering a gentle introduction to the local landscape.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Cañamares?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364026809/hoz-del-conde-and-villaconejos-de-tr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde and Villaconejos de Tr&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 23.9-mile (38.5 km) path featuring significant elevation changes and scenic views through the Hoz del Conde gorge. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086021579/hoz-de-beteta-loop-from-canamares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Beteta Loop from Cañamares&lt;/a&gt;, a 47 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the cycling routes in Cañamares?
A: Cycling routes in Cañamares often follow the Escabas River, which carves out impressive gorges like the Hoz del Conde and features beautiful natural pools. You can also encounter rocky outcrops, extensive forests, and unique 'reddish seas' of wicker fields. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to visit while cycling?
A: Beyond natural wonders, you can explore the 16th-century Iglesia de San Millán in Cañamares. Along the routes, keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Conde&lt;/a&gt; gorge and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Chincha Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The broader Serranía de Cuenca also offers attractions like El Ventano del Diablo viewpoint and the Enchanted City.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086021579/hoz-de-beteta-loop-from-canamares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Beteta Loop from Cañamares&lt;/a&gt; provides a challenging circular experience. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torralba Village Center loop from Albalate de las Nogueras&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 31.7 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cañamares?
A: The warmer months are ideal for touring cycling in Cañamares, especially if you wish to enjoy the natural pools and river 'beaches' for swimming and relaxation. The region's climate allows for pleasant cycling through spring, summer, and early autumn, though summer can be quite warm.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options in Cañamares?
A: While many routes involve varied terrain, easier options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093963639/old-fuertescusa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Fuertescusa Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride. The natural pools along the Escabas River also offer great spots for families to relax after a ride.

Q: Is Cañamares suitable for dog-friendly cycling?
A: Cañamares and the Serranía de Cuenca are generally welcoming to outdoor activities with dogs, given the extensive natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural spaces or near wildlife, and to check specific regulations for any natural parks or reserves you plan to enter.

Q: Where can I find places to relax or have a picnic along the routes?
A: The region offers several spots for relaxation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares natural pool&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot with amenities and natural shade. The 'El Barco' recreational area near the Escabas River also provides facilities for outdoor picnics. For overnight stays, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3763936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping La Dehesa&lt;/a&gt; offers a relaxing base for excursions.

Q: What makes the touring cycling experience in Cañamares unique?
A: The unique charm of touring cycling in Cañamares comes from its diverse landscapes within the Serranía de Cuenca. You'll ride through impressive river gorges, past rocky outcrops, and through extensive forests, often alongside the Escabas River. The region's ecological value and dramatic scenery, including features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta del Infierno&lt;/a&gt;, create a memorable cycling experience.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the touring routes in Cañamares?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cañamares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the river valleys and gorges, and the opportunities to explore areas of significant ecological value.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the region is known for its scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cucurucho Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the surrounding area. Additionally, routes passing through gorges like the Hoz del Conde provide dramatic and expansive views of the landscape.
tips: Wonderful place that you can visit at any time of the year. It is usually more crowded in the summer months since the beach located here is an ideal place to enjoy a refreshing bath in its waters. It also has parking from where you can start your routes.

A beautiful place for all nature lovers. It is a canyon that we can cross by road, one of the most picturesque in Spain. Enjoying the views do not forget to be careful going through the curves next to the rock walls of up to 400 m. Luckily there is little traffic.
The Hoz de Conde formed by the erosion of the Escabas river, is an impressive landscape, very close to the town of Priego.

faqs.question: Where does the Villaconejos de Trabaque y Albalate de las Noqueras por la Hoz del Conde circular route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Cañamares. While specific parking areas are not detailed, Cañamares is a village, and you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for touring cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, offering a smooth cycling experience. However, typical of more challenging routes in the Serranía de Cuenca, some segments may be unpaved tracks. These tracks are generally well-maintained and passable, often shared with official walking routes, leading to various natural attractions.

faqs.question: What natural highlights can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route immerses you in the stunning natural beauty of the Serranía de Cuenca. You'll pass through the impressive Hoz del Conde, known for its dramatic limestone rock formations and lush pine forests. Other notable features include the Estrecho de Priego and the Cañamares natural pool.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as "difficult" and requires a very good level of physical fitness due to its 38.5-kilometer length and 470 meters of elevation gain. It is best suited for experienced touring cyclists looking for a challenging and rewarding ride, rather than beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Hoz del Conde area?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca, including the Hoz del Conde area, is generally pleasant for cycling during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially icy conditions on some tracks.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact cycling route are not provided, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas like the Serranía de Cuenca. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or shared with other users, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Serranía de Cuenca?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and cycle this route within the Serranía de Cuenca. The natural park is generally open for public enjoyment, though visitors are expected to respect local regulations regarding conservation and responsible tourism.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Ruta del Mirador del Rodenal, Sendero de la Degollada, Camino de Santiago de la Lana.BTT, and Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista, among others. These intersections are generally brief, as the route primarily follows its own distinct path.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Cañamares, Villaconejos de Trabaque, and Albalate de las Noqueras. These villages typically offer basic services, including cafes or small restaurants for refreshments and potentially local accommodation options. It's advisable to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a difficult touring cycling route like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to be well-prepared. We recommend bringing plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, and a fully charged phone with the route downloaded for navigation. A helmet is always recommended for safety.

faqs.question: Is there an 'insider tip' for tackling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's "difficult" rating and significant elevation gain, a strategic approach is key. Consider starting your ride early in the morning, especially during warmer months, to avoid the midday heat and take advantage of cooler temperatures. This also allows ample time to complete the route without rushing, letting you fully appreciate the stunning scenery of the Hoz del Conde.
